title: Asyneuma supinum
taxonomy: Asyneuma supinum is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It belongs to the genus Asyneuma, which consists of several species of flowering plants. Asyneuma supinum is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is classified as a perennial herb, characterized by its small, bell-shaped flowers. Asyneuma supinum is often found growing in rocky outcrops and mountain meadows.
uses: Asyneuma supinum is primarily used in traditional medicine, particularly in the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. The plant's leaves and stems are used to make teas and infusions that are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. In some cultures, it is used to treat digestive issues and fever. Additionally, the plant's roots are used in herbal remedies to treat respiratory problems such as bronchitis. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Asyneuma supinum in treating various ailments.

intro: Asyneuma supinum, also known as the alpine asyneuma, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Campanulaceae family, characterized by its small, blue to purple flowers and needle-like leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of A. supinum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, A. supinum is commonly used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
